heading: Badlands National Park; establishment
label: 441 Badlands National Park; establishment
text: When a quantum, satisfactory to the Secretary of the Interior, of the privately owned lands lying within the area hereinafter described shall have been acquired and transferred to the United States for park purposes, without expense to the Federal Treasury, such areas are dedicated and set apart as a national park for the benefit and enjoyment of the people, under the name of the Badlands National Park: , That the State of South Dakota shall have first constructed the highways hereinafter described.Provided
notes: References in TextHereinafter, referred to in text, means act which is classified to sections 441 to 441e of this title. For classification of this Act to the Code, see Tables.Mar. 4, 1929

Change of NameWords “park” and “Park” substituted in text for “monument” and “Monument”, respectively, pursuant to , which is classified to and which redesignated the Badlands National Monument as the Badlands National Park.Pub. L. 95–625, § 611section 441e–1 of this title

Ben Reifel Visitor Center, , , provided in part that:Pub. L. 101–512, title I104 Stat. 1923Nov. 5, 1990“hereafter the Cedar Pass Visitor Center at Badlands National Park, South Dakota, shall be known as the Ben Reifel Visitor Center”.
